text:
To determine how many times Sandra needs to babysit, we first need to find out the total number of eggs required to make 15 Spanish flans. Since each Spanish flan requires 3 eggs, we can calculate the total number of eggs needed as follows:

\[ \text{Total eggs needed} = 15 \text{ flans} \times 3 \text{ eggs/flan} = 45 \text{ eggs} \]

Next, we need to figure out how many times Sandra needs to babysit to get 45 eggs. Since she gets 9 eggs each time she babysits, we can calculate the number of babysitting sessions required by dividing the total number of eggs needed by the number of eggs she gets per babysitting session:

\[ \text{Number of babysitting sessions needed} = \frac{45 \text{ eggs}}{9 \text{ eggs/session}} = 5 \text{ sessions} \]

Therefore, Sandra needs to babysit 5 times to get enough eggs to make 15 Spanish flans.
